extracted.title: COMPLETED: Clementsville Compressor Station (ClemSEG) Eff. Eve Cycle for Gas Day March 28, 2026
extracted.description:
Columbia Gulf Transmission, LLC (CGT) notifies customers that maintenance on Clementsville Compressor Station unit 209 is now complete. As a result, the backhaul capacity through ClemSEG will be reinstated to Total Capacity effective Evening Cycle for Gas Day March 28, 2026.

Please contact your Customer Services Representative with any questions.

***Previous Posting***

Columbia Gulf Transmission LLC (CGT) notifies customers of upcoming maintenance on Clementsville Compressor Station Unit 209 on Tuesday, March 3, 2026 through Friday, March 27, 2026. As a result, the backhaul capacity through ClemSEG will be reduced to a level between 1,850,000 Dth and 2,000,000 Dth effective Timely Cycle for Gas Day Tuesday, March 3, 2026.

CGT anticipates this restriction may result in curtailments to Interruptible, Secondary Firm, and Primary Firm services. Please see the details provided below for more information.

ClemSEG
- Estimated Capacity Setting: 1,850,000 – 2,000,000 Dth
- Design Capacity: 2,344,656 Dth
- Posted Percentage: 79-85%

Due to the potential impact to firm service, CGT encourages customers to monitor the scheduled volumes on the Operationally Available Capacity screen on Informational Postings, as nominations can fluctuate based on market conditions.

Reservation Charge Credits (RCCs) will be determined per the process set forth in the General Terms and Conditions, Section 40 of CGT’s FERC Gas Tariff (Tariff). By means of this notice, CGT is establishing the 7-day Average Usage Quantity to be the period of December 30, 2025 – January 5, 2026, pursuant to the provisions in the Tariff.

Please contact your Customer Services Representative with any questions.
